## Sub-domains (communities)

| ID | Size | Cohesion | Description |
|----|------|----------|-------------|
[One row per kept community (top 10 by size). ID + size + cohesion: mechanical. Cohesion shown as clustering-quality info (0-1 scale, higher = more semantically cohesive), not a selection criterion. Description: short LLM sentence (~10-15 words) naming the inferred role from member node labels and typical `source_file`. Example: "Business service layer — orchestrates operations on rides and customers".]

## Structural hubs (gods)

| Node | Source | Degree | Inferred role |
|------|--------|--------|---------------|
[Top 10 gods. Node label + Source (namespace/path from `graph.json` if available, else owning community) + degree: mechanical. The **Source** column is essential to disambiguate when the same label appears several times. Inferred role: short LLM sentence explaining why this node is a hub.]

## Detected anti-patterns (surprises)

**If "Surprising Connections" has entries:**

[For each kept surprise, a block:

**`<node label>`** (community `<id>` ↔ `<id>`)
> `<raw why from graphify>`

LLM sentence reinterpreting it in context: what does it concretely mean for an agent modifying this code? Propagation risk? Legitimate bridge to preserve? Unfinished refactor?

Repeat per surprise.]

**If "None detected" (Suggested Questions fallback, dense graphs):**

> No surprise formally detected by graphify (dense graph, clustering found no unusual bridge). The cross-community bridges below come from Suggested Questions and deserve manual verification.

[For each god node appearing in a "Why does `X` connect Community A to B, C, D?" question:

**`<node label>`** — bridge between Community `<A>` and `<B, C, D>`
> High betweenness centrality (`<value>`). Modifying this node may propagate impact across several sub-domains.

Repeat for the 3-5 most relevant (skip questions on isolated nodes like `name`, `version`).]

## Additional analysis — <tool name>

## Notes for the agent

[LLM, 3-5 lines of practical advice for approaching this project:
- What to read first (entry file, API entry point, project README if any)
- Which nodes/files are critical hubs to understand before any structural change
- Which anti-patterns to watch when proposing changes
- Inferred conventions (framework, architectural style, dominant language, presence of tests, etc.)]
